description: Ephemeral OAuth session state for an authenticated account, held locally by the appview. Tokens are never written to a PDS: this lexicon exists so the appview treats session state uniformly with every other panproto-shaped value (identity resolution caches, DPoP nonces, firehose cursors). Token lifecycle transitions (issue, refresh, revoke) are modeled as lenses over this schema's w-instances.

main.description: The full session state for one (did, pdsUrl) pair. Keyed by the literal 'self' so the session is a singleton per logged-in account.

did.description: DID of the authenticated account. Session ownership boundary.

pdsUrl.description: Base URL of the Personal Data Server that issued the tokens. Tokens are PDS-scoped in atproto OAuth; a refresh must hit the same host.

accessJwt.description: Opaque access token. Semantically sensitive: never rendered to UI or logged at any level above trace.

refreshJwt.description: Opaque refresh token. Used to mint a new access token when the current one expires. Never rendered or logged.

dpopPrivateKeyJwk.description: DPoP private key, serialized as a JSON Web Key string. The appview signs DPoP proofs with this key for every request. Serialized as a string (not a nested object) so the schema node is a primitive and the entire key material round-trips as one opaque unit.

dpopNonce.description: Latest DPoP nonce returned by the PDS on a 401 or in a Use-DPoP-Nonce header. Updated on every request that yields a new nonce; the panproto lens for 'record nonce' rewrites exactly this field.

tokenType.description: OAuth 2.0 token_type. For atproto this is always 'DPoP'; carried explicitly for forward compatibility with other bindings.

scope.description: Space-delimited scope string the PDS returned on issuance. Surfaces what the token is allowed to do (e.g. 'atproto transition:generic').

handle.description: User-facing handle at the time of issuance. Cached here so UI can render it without re-resolving identity; authoritative source remains PLC / the identity layer.

issuedAt.description: When this session was created or last refreshed.

expiresAt.description: Access-token expiry. The appview must refresh before this instant; after this instant the session is unusable without a refresh_jwt round-trip.

refreshExpiresAt.description: Optional absolute expiry of the refresh_jwt. If present and passed, the session is fully expired and the user must re-authenticate.
